title: The One With The Giant Poking Device

jokes.text: See, that is the problem with invisible dentists. It's very unsettling to see all those metal tools just flying around.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions she can't see her dentist (context suggests fear/avoidance)
jokes.punchline: Rachel deliberately misinterprets 'can't see him' as literally invisible dentist
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke with good visual imagery. Economy is strong - gets to the punchline quickly and efficiently.

jokes.text: Because every time I go to the dentist, somebody dies.
jokes.setup: Normal question about why she can't go to her dentist
jokes.punchline: Completely unexpected and dark reasoning that defies logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe character comedy - her unique brand of magical thinking delivered matter-of-factly. High originality and character integration.

jokes.text: That is so weird. Every time I go to the dentist... I look down the hygienist's blouse.
jokes.setup: Joey starts to relate to Phoebe's 'every time I go to the dentist' setup
jokes.punchline: Completely different and inappropriate revelation
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey horndog humor. Good structure but predictable for the character.

jokes.text: First, it was my Aunt Mary. And then there was John, my mailman. And then my cowboy friend, Albino Bob.
jokes.setup: Providing evidence for her dentist death curse
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and bizarre final example 'Albino Bob'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect Phoebe-esque detail. 'Albino Bob' is oddly specific and memorable.

jokes.text: That's why I take such good care of my teeth. It's not about oral hygiene. I floss to save lives.
jokes.setup: Explaining her dental care routine
jokes.punchline: Reframing mundane hygiene as heroic life-saving activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy that takes Phoebe's magical thinking to its logical conclusion. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Tell that to them. Oh, you can't! They're dead!
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting it's just coincidence
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact reminder that prevents any counter-argument
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good dark humor. Great economy - punchline lands immediately.

jokes.text: Like you don't already have everything.
jokes.setup: Customer asks for napkin
jokes.punchline: Gunther's bitter sarcasm about customer's perceived privilege
jokes.explanation: Classic Gunther - bitter and passive-aggressive. Good character moment but not spectacular.

jokes.text: But if you're my next victim... don't come back as a poltergeist who sucks me into the TV set.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to go to dentist despite death curse
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific supernatural revenge scenario
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe weirdness with specific pop culture reference. Slightly wordy but entertaining.

jokes.text: Although, don't feel like you can't visit.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's poltergeist warning
jokes.punchline: Monica politely encouraging ghostly visitation
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up that maintains the absurd premise while being characteristically polite.

jokes.text: I was thinking about that. I think the best way would be... to not.
jokes.setup: How to tell Chandler about Janice cheating
jokes.punchline: Complete avoidance presented as a strategy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Joey's characteristic conflict avoidance. Well-structured pause.

jokes.text: Wait until I go to the dentist. Maybe I'll kill him.
jokes.setup: Discussion about news killing Chandler
jokes.punchline: Phoebe offering her death curse as solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that applies her earlier absurd premise to current situation. Perfect character logic.

jokes.text: Ugly Naked Guy's using his new hammock. It's like a Play-Doh fat factory.
jokes.setup: Observing neighbor across the way
jokes.punchline: Vivid and gross visual comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comedy but somewhat crude. Play-Doh comparison is creative but not deeply character-specific.

jokes.text: Or just look out!
jokes.setup: Warning about dangers while she's at dentist
jokes.punchline: Giving up on specific warnings and going completely general
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Phoebe's scattered thinking. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: [Awkward pause as Phoebe starts emotional goodbye]
jokes.setup: Phoebe beginning to say goodbye as if going to die
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable beat as others realize what she's doing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Phoebe's commitment to the bit, but relies on timing.

jokes.text: What did they do? They painted over the word 'sapien,' for one thing.
jokes.setup: Kids messed up the Homo sapien display
jokes.punchline: The obvious and juvenile vandalism
jokes.explanation: Predictable juvenile humor setup. Works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Then they rearranged the figures. Let's just leave it at that.
jokes.setup: Describing the vandalism
jokes.punchline: Ross's uncomfortable refusal to elaborate on sexual positioning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's prudishness. The implication works better than explicit description.

jokes.text: I just asked Monica... because I know how empty her life is.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he asked Monica to babysit
jokes.punchline: Brutal and casual assessment of his sister's life
jokes.explanation: Good sibling cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. Monica's presence makes it more awkward.

jokes.text: If the Homo sapiens were, in fact, 'homo' sapiens... is that why they're extinct?
jokes.setup: Scientific question about human evolution
jokes.punchline: Joey's complete misunderstanding of the term
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey stupidity that feels authentic to character. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm not judging.
jokes.setup: Being corrected about homo sapiens meaning humans
jokes.punchline: Joey's fake tolerance for what he thinks is sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Joey doubling down on his misunderstanding while trying to seem progressive.

jokes.text: [Baby Ben hits his head - physical comedy moment]
jokes.setup: Monica playing airplane game with baby
jokes.punchline: Baby accidentally getting hurt during innocent play
jokes.explanation: Physical setup for ongoing plot. More plot device than joke, though there's some dark humor in babysitting gone wrong.

jokes.text: Nothing, because we're not telling him. / We're not? / No. / All right, I like that.
jokes.setup: Deciding what to tell Ross about baby's injury
jokes.punchline: Rachel's immediate enthusiasm for deception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's willingness to avoid confrontation. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Well, push it in! Push it in! / I cannot push it in!
jokes.setup: Discovering bump on baby's head
jokes.punchline: Absurd suggestion to just push the bump back in
jokes.explanation: Good panic humor with absurd medical logic. The desperation makes it funny.

jokes.text: The second Ross walks in... take him to your bedroom, and do whatever it is you do... that makes him go... [gesture]
jokes.setup: Need distraction when Ross returns
jokes.punchline: Monica's awkward reference to Rachel and Ross's sex life
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor from Monica's discomfort discussing sex. The gesture adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: A hat! Yes! We need a hat! Where are we gonna find a tiny little hat?
jokes.setup: Alternative solution to hiding the bump
jokes.punchline: The logical problem with the hat solution - need baby-sized hat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd problem-solving. The specificity of 'tiny little hat' adds humor.

jokes.text: I'll get Rainy Day Bear! / Because he'll know what to do? / Oh, my God. You're a genius!
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting her stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: Rachel initially thinking Monica means the bear will solve it, then realizing it's for the hat
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with Rachel's initial confusion. Good panic logic escalation.

jokes.text: It's just like a bloodbath in here today!
jokes.setup: Phoebe returning from dentist
jokes.punchline: Dramatic description suggesting her death curse struck again
jokes.explanation: Good callback that plays on expectations. Phoebe's dramatic language fits her character.

jokes.text: Get her candy. Get her gum. Girls love gum. / That's a good idea. 'Dear Janice: Have a Hubba Bubba birthday.'
jokes.setup: Joey's advice about gifts for women
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically imagining the ridiculous card message
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay with Joey's simplistic advice and Chandler's sarcastic wit. Quotable line.

jokes.text: You know what you should do? You should get her one of those... barium enemas.
jokes.setup: Joey offering serious gift advice
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate medical procedure as romantic gift
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist suggestion. The medical specificity makes it funnier than a generic procedure.

jokes.text: Those are dead serious. And you know she'll never get one for herself.
jokes.setup: Explaining why barium enema is a good gift
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely sincere justification of the absurd suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent follow-up that commits to the absurd premise. Joey's earnest gift logic is perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You can't! You can't. You can't buy her pearls. You just can't. You can't, You can't, you can't!
jokes.setup: Trying to stop Chandler from buying pearls
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasingly desperate and inarticulate repetition
jokes.explanation: Good panic escalation but somewhat repetitive. Shows Joey's inability to articulate under pressure.

jokes.text: Bet that barium enema doesn't sound so bad now.
jokes.setup: Chandler learning about Janice's affair
jokes.punchline: Darkly ironic reference to earlier gift suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes the earlier absurd suggestion as grimly appropriate. Perfect timing.

jokes.text: It makes more sense as an ensemble. Besides, it takes the focus off the hat.
jokes.setup: Apparently additional items have been added to baby's disguise
jokes.punchline: Monica's design logic for what's clearly become an over-the-top costume
jokes.explanation: Great Monica character moment - her perfectionist instincts applied to baby disguise. Visual comedy we can imagine.

jokes.text: Oh! You're alive! You're alive!
jokes.setup: Phoebe seeing everyone after dentist visit
jokes.punchline: Her genuine surprise that the death curse didn't activate
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phoebe's genuine belief in her curse. Her relief feels authentic to character.

jokes.text: Monica bang! / Did he just say, 'Monica bang'? / Oh, my God! He's gonna rat me out!
jokes.setup: Baby saying his first clear words
jokes.punchline: The words perfectly incriminate Monica for the head injury
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot callback with perfect comedic timing. Baby's 'testimony' is both adorable and incriminating.

jokes.text: Monica bang! Everybody bang! Ben bang! Rachel bang! Bang, Rachel! Bang!
jokes.setup: Trying to make 'bang' seem normal by getting everyone to say it
jokes.punchline: The plan backfiring as they sound increasingly manic and suspicious
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of panic. The more they try to normalize it, the weirder it gets. Good physical comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah. You know, if it's not a headboard, it's just not worth it.
jokes.setup: Monica apparently hitting her head during sex demonstration
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry assessment of her sexual preferences
jokes.explanation: Good character humor with Monica's matter-of-fact delivery about sexual mishap. Well-structured punchline.

jokes.text: How's my Bing-a-ling?
jokes.setup: Janice greeting Chandler
jokes.punchline: Her characteristic awful pun on his last name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice character moment - terrible pun delivered with complete confidence. Low craft but high character accuracy.

jokes.text: Maybe it's because I was just fooling around with my ex. / Oh, no. That was you!
jokes.setup: Chandler's leading questions about confession
jokes.punchline: Janice casually revealing another affair, Chandler's horror at multiple incidents
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the situation worse. Chandler's realization adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: In the park? / No. In his office. How many kisses were there?
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to understand the scope
jokes.punchline: The revelation of multiple locations and Chandler's growing panic
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation with good pacing. Chandler's investigative questions reveal worse news.

jokes.text: It's the receipt. / I'll take it.
jokes.setup: Chandler hyperventilating into what he thinks is a bag
jokes.punchline: Janice matter-of-factly taking her shopping receipt back
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with practical punchline. Janice's casual attitude contrasts with Chandler's panic.

jokes.text: if you won't tell him about our little bonking incident. / Number one, I don't think Ben understands the concept of bribery. And number two, I... / What? / You said, 'number two.'
jokes.setup: Ross making logical points about Monica's plan
jokes.punchline: Monica's juvenile fixation on bathroom humor despite the serious conversation
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor that shows Monica's nervousness. Character-appropriate but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: What did you do, take him whaling?
jokes.setup: Ross noticing Ben seems tired/worn out
jokes.punchline: Absurd suggestion of extremely strenuous baby activity
jokes.explanation: Good hyperbolic humor. Whaling is absurdly inappropriate for a baby but perfectly expresses the observation.

jokes.text: That's right. That's what I'd sound like if I exploded.
jokes.setup: Covering for 'Monica bang' revelation
jokes.punchline: Monica's absurd explanation that she's demonstrating explosion sounds
jokes.explanation: Creative cover story that's absurd enough to be believable as Monica weirdness. Good quick thinking.

jokes.text: The curse is broken! I called everybody I know... and everyone is alive!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's relief about the dentist visit
jokes.punchline: Her systematic verification that nobody died
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phoebe's thorough approach to her magical thinking. Character-consistent logic.

jokes.text: Ugly Naked Guy looks awfully still.
jokes.setup: Phoebe celebrating the end of her death curse
jokes.punchline: Immediate suggestion that someone might actually be dead
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic timing that undercuts Phoebe's celebration. Great setup for next sequence.

jokes.text: My God, I killed another one! The curse is getting stronger too, to bring down something that big.
jokes.setup: Realizing Ugly Naked Guy might be dead
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's immediate acceptance and size-based power assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe logic - accepting responsibility and analyzing the 'upgrade' in her curse power. Perfect character beat.

jokes.text: And tell them what? The naked guy we stare at isn't moving?
jokes.setup: Suggestion to call authorities about UNG
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out how creepy their surveillance sounds when stated plainly
jokes.explanation: Great self-awareness joke that highlights how weird their behavior actually is. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: You know how we save all those chopsticks... for no reason when we get Chinese food? Well, now we got a reason.
jokes.setup: Need to poke UNG to check if he's alive
jokes.punchline: Finally finding a use for pointlessly saved chopsticks
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about universal behavior. Great structure building to practical application.

jokes.text: It serves me right for buying that 12-pack of condoms.
jokes.setup: Chandler lamenting his romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Blaming his optimistic bulk purchase for the relationship curse
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with relatable superstition about tempting fate. Classic Chandler.

jokes.text: And now I can't even return them, because she choked on the receipt!
jokes.setup: Continuing complaint about condom purchase
jokes.punchline: Practical problem created by earlier hyperventilation scene
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects earlier physical comedy to current problem. Solid structure.

jokes.text: No offense, but I've always thought of Ben as a fairly bumpy-headed child.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about bump on Ben's head
jokes.punchline: Rachel's casual assessment that Ben was already bumpy-headed
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that deflects blame while being accidentally insulting about the baby.

jokes.text: I watched Monica bang his head against that thing!
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her babysitting vigilance
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing Monica's guilt while defending herself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Rachel's self-defense backfires. Classic panic revelation structure.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I wrecked your baby!
jokes.setup: Ross describing Ben's apparent neurological problems
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified realization and casual term 'wrecked'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Monica's dramatic guilt. 'Wrecked your baby' is a funny way to phrase it.

jokes.text: I hope it's still funny when you're in hell.
jokes.setup: Others laughing at the baby situation
jokes.punchline: Ross's dark threat about eternal punishment
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that shows Ross's protective father rage. Characteristically melodramatic.

jokes.text: The way I feel about you... it's like I finally understand... what Lionel Richie's been singing about.
jokes.setup: Chandler expressing deep romantic feelings
jokes.punchline: Comparing love to understanding Lionel Richie lyrics
jokes.explanation: Great romantic comedy line that's both sincere and funny. Perfect Chandler mix of emotion and pop culture reference.

jokes.text: I'll settle for half your heart. I'll settle for a quarter. I'll settle for a little, tiny... A ventricle.
jokes.setup: Janice saying he deserves someone's whole heart
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly desperate and anatomically specific negotiations
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with good anatomical specificity. Shows Chandler's desperation while being genuinely clever.

jokes.text: You can't leave! I have your shoe!
jokes.setup: Janice leaving the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pathetic attempt to keep her using footwear
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Chandler's desperation. Simple but effective in showing how low he'll go.

jokes.text: Rachel has those in burgundy.
jokes.setup: Chandler's emotional devastation after breakup
jokes.punchline: Monica's completely inappropriate fashion observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - missing the emotional significance for fashion details. Great timing and character work.

jokes.text: Fortunately, I have experience in that area.
jokes.setup: Discussion about maneuvering something big and long
jokes.punchline: Joey's sexual innuendo about his own anatomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey sexual innuendo. Predictable but character-appropriate. Low originality but good character fit.

jokes.text: What are you, 12 today?
jokes.setup: Joey's sexual comment
jokes.punchline: Monica's exasperated response treating him like a child
jokes.explanation: Good straight-person response to Joey's innuendo. Monica's maternal exasperation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What we're about to have here is a dead, fat guy on a stick!
jokes.setup: Preparing to poke Ugly Naked Guy
jokes.punchline: Joey's crude but vivid description of the potential outcome
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy description. Crude but effectively paints the absurd picture of their plan.

jokes.text: He's alive! He's alive! / And yet we're still poking him.
jokes.setup: Successfully confirming UNG is alive
jokes.punchline: Monica's observation that they haven't stopped poking him
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about continuing the action after achieving the goal. Monica's practical observation.

jokes.text: Now he's showing us his poking device.
jokes.setup: UNG responding angrily to being poked
jokes.punchline: Joey's euphemistic description of UNG's angry gesture
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comedy with Joey's characteristic euphemistic interpretation. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: Hey, that's never gonna make it all the way over here, buddy!
jokes.setup: UNG's angry gesture from across the courtyard
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical assessment of the distance involved
jokes.explanation: Good practical Chandler response to the absurd situation. Nice way to end the episode on a confident note.
